nl_query: For 2002 store purchases tied to marketing campaigns, show the top 10 household income bands and warehouses that had positive stock for the sold items on the sale date, ranked by store net sales and distinct customers.
expected_measures: sum store net paid; distinct store customers; units sold
semantic_focus: Warehouse is inferred through stock availability for the sold item on the sale date; do not invent a direct store-to-warehouse fulfillment key.

nl_query: Which web properties and site page types drove the most online net paid in 2002 for female preferred customers in each income band when a marketing campaign was attached?
expected_measures: sum online net paid; distinct online customers
semantic_focus: Tests web property plus site page plus promotion plus customer income and gender context.

nl_query: For mail-order purchases in 2002, rank warehouses and delivery carriers by net paid with delivery for high-income households, and show distinct customers and distinct orders.
expected_measures: sum net paid with delivery; distinct customers; distinct orders
semantic_focus: Warehouse is directly available on mail-order purchases; income comes through customer household profile.

nl_query: For customers in California during 2002, compare promoted online, mail-order, and store purchase revenue by income band and customer gender.
expected_measures: sum net paid by channel; distinct customers by channel
semantic_focus: Requires channel union without mixing billing customer keys incorrectly.

nl_query: For 2002 online purchases with campaigns, which web properties and site page types had the highest refunded net paid, and what share of promoted online net paid was refunded?
expected_measures: sum promoted online net paid; sum refunded net paid; refund rate
semantic_focus: Tests purchase-to-return alignment and ratio denominator clarity.

nl_query: For store transactions in 2002, compare net sales and return amounts by income band, product category, and campaign purpose for customers with known household profiles.
expected_measures: sum store net paid; sum return amount; return rate
semantic_focus: Tests store sales and returns with campaign and income segmentation.

nl_query: For each warehouse in 2002, which merchandise categories had positive stock on days when online promoted sales occurred, and what were online customers and online net paid for those categories?
expected_measures: distinct online customers; sum online net paid; stock days
semantic_focus: Warehouse is inventory availability, not online fulfillment.

nl_query: For each marketing campaign purpose in 2002, compare customer counts and net paid across store, online, and mail-order channels, split by household income band.
expected_measures: distinct customers; sum net paid by channel
semantic_focus: Tests consistent customer identity across all three sales channels.

nl_query: For online purchases in 2002, show net paid and distinct customers by site page type, web property, and customer state for promoted orders only.
expected_measures: sum online net paid; distinct customers
semantic_focus: Tests web-site context and current customer address geography.

nl_query: Which store locations in Tennessee sold the most Jewelry items in 2002, and how did the customer count split by household income band?
expected_measures: sum store net paid; distinct customers
semantic_focus: Tests store geography versus customer income segmentation.

nl_query: For mail-order sales in 2002, which mailer pages and campaign purposes produced the highest net paid among preferred customers, split by income band?
expected_measures: sum mail-order net paid; distinct customers
semantic_focus: Tests catalog page, campaign, preferred customer, and income joins.

nl_query: For mail-order purchases in 2002, show support centers, delivery carriers, and warehouses by net profit for customers in each income band.
expected_measures: sum net profit; distinct orders; distinct customers
semantic_focus: Tests fulfillment and support dimensions together.

nl_query: For 2002 store purchases, which campaign names produced the largest coupon savings and highest net paid by product category and customer gender?
expected_measures: sum coupon amount; sum net paid; distinct customers
semantic_focus: Tests discount metrics and customer demographics at store line-item grain.

nl_query: For online purchases in 2002, compare first-time customers versus returning customers by income band, web property, and merchandise category.
expected_measures: distinct customers; sum online net paid
semantic_focus: Requires comparing first_sale_calendar_day_ref with the purchase year.

nl_query: For store sales in 2002, find merchandise categories where stores sold items on days when no warehouse had positive stock for that item, and report affected customers and net paid.
expected_measures: distinct customers; sum net paid; count sale lines
semantic_focus: Tests anti-join logic between store sales and inventory availability.

nl_query: For 2002 online sales, rank return reasons by refunded amount and original online net paid, split by income band and web property.
expected_measures: sum refunded amount; sum original net paid; refund share
semantic_focus: Tests returns joined back to online purchase/customer context.

nl_query: Which campaign purposes in 2002 had the highest store return rate among preferred customers, grouped by store state and income band?
expected_measures: return rate; sum sales; sum returns; distinct customers
semantic_focus: Tests campaign/store/customer/income/return alignment.

nl_query: In 2002, how many customers bought through both online and mail-order channels under campaigns, and how does their spend split by income band?
expected_measures: distinct overlap customers; online net paid; mail-order net paid
semantic_focus: Tests customer overlap across channels without double-counting.

nl_query: For items promoted in 2002, show warehouse stock availability, online net paid, and store net paid by merchandise category and campaign name.
expected_measures: stock quantity; online net paid; store net paid
semantic_focus: Tests combining inventory with sales channels through item and date.

nl_query: For mail-order purchases in 2002, which support centers and warehouses had the highest return amount by return reason and income band?
expected_measures: sum return amount; return count; original net paid
semantic_focus: Tests mail-order return attribution to support and warehouse context.

nl_query: For customers from Texas in 2002, compare online, store, and mail-order distinct buyers and net paid by household income band.
expected_measures: distinct buyers by channel; sum net paid
semantic_focus: Tests current customer address plus cross-channel aggregation.

nl_query: For 2002 campaigns, compare online net paid by campaign channel flags such as email, direct mail, TV, and radio, split by web property and product category.
expected_measures: sum online net paid; distinct customers
semantic_focus: Tests promotion channel flag semantics and web property joins.

nl_query: For store purchases in 2002, show net paid by hour of day, campaign purpose, and income band for customers with known household profiles.
expected_measures: sum store net paid; distinct customers
semantic_focus: Tests time-of-day dimension plus campaign and income segmentation.

nl_query: Among customers who returned an online item in 2002, which web properties and campaign purposes had the highest original net paid and refunded amount by gender?
expected_measures: original net paid; refunded amount; distinct returners
semantic_focus: Tests customer returner cohort with web and campaign dimensions.

nl_query: For 2002, compare promoted store sales, online sales, mail-order sales, and warehouse stock availability by product category and household income band, showing distinct customers and total net paid per channel.
expected_measures: distinct customers; net paid by channel; stock quantity
semantic_focus: Broad context stress test; warehouse is inventory availability rather than direct fulfillment for every channel.
